- you must reduce top moyo

g10-j8-k7-g8 or
g10-j8-j7-h8

C[dmg: I don't think pushing white here is a good idea. You're weakening R10. For pushing R5 would probably have been better. But I would have played O4/O3 Getting territory and a chance for Q10 to escape easily while keeping separated the white stones.
C[dmg: The biggest problem here is that you're leaving the cut at Q4.
C[dmg: And now you need to fix it leaving black stones nearly dead.
C[dmg: You should prefer putting pressure on D14 group. Maybe G10 or C10 (making thickness toward your D4 stone
C[dmg: Same comment than before. Pincer at C8 instead?
C[dmg: See, you're adding the C10 stone now. Leaving the cut at D5
C[dmg: Starting now white is exploiting this cut.
C[dmg: H4 looks better here. G4 protects the cut.
C[dmg: Bad shape, white will cut this stone.

C[dmg: Did you read that?
Don't forget:
Keima to attack
Kosumi to separate or to escape
Tobi to extend/protect or escape
Attaching is for making eyes/shape.
maproom: I certainly did not read it.  As White in a handicap game, if you read everything assuming perfect play, you just have to resign.
dmg: Yeah I know. But I commented to fast :p. It looked weird for a black move :)
C[dmg: You're helping him connecting. Try something else (R16 for example) until it becomes really interesting.
C[dmg: Humm why not on the other side (C13 for instance). Moreover you still have room to escape near J9.
C[dmg: Throw in at D8 first ensure you to capture at least F8. (the whole group if it's going straight. bD8-wD9-bE9-wD8-bB7-wA3-bC7.

maproom: ""bD8-wD9-bE9-wD8-bB7-wA3-bC7.""
W won't A3, he'll C7. Neither of them works, but C7 is much more likely to lead to fun complications :)

C[maproom: I had read that this does not work.  But resigning seemed to be the only alternative, so I tried it anyway.  If it does work (killing B's b2 group and saving my d8 group), then B's big h11 group is in serious trouble.
maproom: this comment is meant to be attached to move 145, at d2
dmg: Well that does work. From here bE4-wF3-bE3-wF2 :)
